mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates applied to equity valuations, compressing forward S&P 500 returns. Each 25 basis point increase in FEDFUNDS over a 3-month window predicts negative 20-day forward returns as market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.41% as of 2018-01-01 in tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window post-trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market tightness that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in stronger demand.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in < 52% of months with PAYEMS growth > 150k, or correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth and 5-day forward returns drops below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Declining retail sales (RSAFS) combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals demand contraction and recession risk. When both conditions hold simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 20 trading days as equity markets reprice earnings downward in anticipation of profit margin compression.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 2 months
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 492,003 million and UNRATE at 4.1% as of 2018-01-01
regime_hint: early-stage demand shock or recession onset
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of co-triggered instances (RSAFS down AND UNRATE up), or joint signal produces Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% month-over-month signals supply-side expansion and capacity utilization gains that support profit margins and equity valuations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 60-day forward returns as markets recognize sustained productivity improvements.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 107.24 index points as of 2018-01-01
regime_hint: any regime with positive supply momentum
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 54% of months with INDPRO growth > 1%, or rolling 3-year Rank IC between INDPRO growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,100 thousand units signals weakening residential demand and forward-looking economic weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day S&P 500 returns as markets anticipate spillover effects to construction employment and consumer wealth.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,192 thousand units as of 2017-12-01
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of months when HOUST < 1,100k, or Rank IC between HOUST and 20-day forward returns exceeds -0.02 in absolute value over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year combined with FEDFUNDS below 2.0% signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns as markets anticipate policy accommodation and equity risk premium compression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 249.25 and FEDFUNDS at 1.41% as of 2018-01-01
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 48% of co-triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and forward earnings risk. In post-shock recovery regimes, this threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns as markets reprice growth expectations and corporate hiring intentions downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of months when UNRATE > 4.5%, or Rank IC between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns exceeds -0.03 in absolute value over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly combined with retail sales momentum (RSAFS) both accelerating signals broad-based demand expansion. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day S&P 500 returns as markets recognize synchronized growth across consumption and output.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2.5% AND RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17,272.47 billion and RSAFS at 492,003 million as of 2018-01-01
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion in mid-to-late cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 48% of co-triggered instances, or joint signal Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.
